explanation: This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the STANDBY state. This is captured by the implication "standby -> F(state_is_STANDBY)" which states that whenever standby is true, eventually the autopilot will reach the STANDBY state. The "G" operator ensures this property holds globally, meaning it must hold at all times.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state and the pilot is in control (standby), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state.  The property uses the "until" (U) operator to ensure that the autopilot stays in the TRANSITION state until the standby condition is met, at which point it transitions to the STANDBY state.

This captures the requirement that the autopilot should be able to change states from TRANSITION to STANDBY upon pilot control, without specifying how quickly this transition should occur.

It is important to note that the property does not specify how long it will take for the transition to occur, only that it will eventually happen.
